text.title: Tools, Services, Agent Workflow, and Gotchas
text.summary: CLI tools, platform services, agent workflow guidance, and critical failure modes for LLM-powered conversational AI.

list.item: anthropic CLI — test Claude models for conversational AI prototyping; pip install anthropic
openai CLI — test GPT-4o for conversational AI; pip install openai
rasa CLI — train and test open-source NLU + dialogue models; pip install rasa
botium-cli — automated conversation testing framework; npm install -g botium-cli
deepgram CLI — speech-to-text for voice pipeline testing; npm install @deepgram/sdk
gcloud (Dialogflow CX) — deploy and test conversation agents; cloud.google.com/sdk
Anthropic Claude API (SaaS) — primary LLM; system prompts, multi-turn, streaming, tool use; REST API
Dialogflow CX (Google SaaS) — enterprise conversational platform with LLM integration; REST API
Amazon Lex v2 (SaaS) — AWS-native; integrates with Lambda for fulfilment; REST API
Voiceflow (SaaS) — prototyping + production deployment; supports LLM nodes; REST API
ElevenLabs (SaaS) — TTS for voice output; agent can generate audio previews; REST API
Deepgram (SaaS) — ASR with low latency; good for real-time voice pipelines; REST API
Botium (OSS/SaaS) — automated conversation regression testing; CLI + API

section.title: Agent Workflow

rule.statement: A Claude subagent is suited to designing, testing, and auditing LLM conversational AI systems. When an agent generates the system prompt AND also acts as the conversational AI in testing, it will validate its own outputs favorably — use a separate model instance for red-teaming.
rule.rationale: An agent evaluating its own system prompt has a systematic bias toward finding it adequate. Adversarial testing requires a separate, unconstrained instance with no system prompt context from the target.

section.reference: Agent prompt: generate production system prompt with guardrails
Agent prompt: red-team a system prompt for adversarial vectors

antipattern.description: Agent-generated system prompts with 2000+ tokens due to verbose explanation and example inclusion
antipattern.reason: Token bloat increases latency and cost in multi-turn conversations. System prompt should be under 500 tokens. Move examples to few-shot turns, not system prompt body.

antipattern.description: Agent designs conversation flows for only the happy path
antipattern.reason: Explicitly prompt for error recovery, ambiguity handling, and out-of-scope scenarios. Happy-path-only designs fail on the first real user interaction that deviates from the expected flow.

antipattern.description: Agent designs tool calls without accounting for network round-trip latency per call
antipattern.reason: Each function/tool call in an LLM-powered voice system adds a full network round-trip. For real-time voice, batch tool calls or pre-fetch data to avoid visible pauses.

antipattern.description: Agent runs identical system prompt without regression tests after any modification
antipattern.reason: System prompt changes can break existing dialogue flows non-obviously. Run automated regression tests (Botium) on the full dialogue script corpus after every system prompt change.
